name: test-guard
description: Prevents AI-generated tests from passing-for-the-wrong-reasons — over-mocked, tautological, premise-tested, lazy-assertion tests that pass without proving behavior. Seven checks: skip-detection, mocks-the-system-under-test, tautological-assertions, boundary-coverage, fake-dependencies, lazy-assertions, order/timing-flakiness. Pairs with test-driven-development (the methodology); this skill enforces that the resulting tests actually test something.

positive_triggers.input: AI wrote tests for the new auth helper. Before merge, audit them for tautologies and over-mocking.
positive_triggers.reason: Pre-merge test quality audit on AI-generated tests → matches Use when 'reviewing AI-generated test code before merge'

positive_triggers.input: Test suite is 100% green but our prod bug slipped through. Where's the silent-green failure mode?
positive_triggers.reason: Silent-green failure mode is the canonical test-guard domain → matches Use when 'test suite is green but production bugs are escaping'

positive_triggers.input: Establish a CI gate that fails PRs on lazy assertions and over-mocking.
positive_triggers.reason: Per-PR CI gate setup on test quality → matches Use when 'establish a per-PR test-quality gate'

positive_triggers.input: Postmortem says the bug slipped because the test was tautological. Teach me what to look for next time.
positive_triggers.reason: Post-incident learning on tautological tests → matches Use when 'after a passing-test incident'

negative_triggers.input: Write a failing test for the new feature, then minimum code to pass.
negative_triggers.reason: TDD discipline execution → matches NOT for 'writing the test first per TDD' → test-driven-development routes here

negative_triggers.input: Production p99 regressed to 1s — find the bottleneck.
negative_triggers.reason: Performance regression → matches NOT for 'production-bug root-cause hunt' → debugging-and-error-recovery or performance-optimization routes here

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer runs test-guard against an AI-generated test file for an auth helper. The tests are 100% green.
expected_behavior: §1 Each test gets a 1-sentence behavior claim: 'this test verifies that the auth helper returns null when password is missing' (example)
§2 CHECK 1 (skip): grep for skip patterns in test files; any hit gets severity HIGH; flagged test fails the gate
§2 CHECK 2 (mocks the SUT): for each jest.mock call, verify the mock isn't replacing the function the test claims to test; if so, severity HIGH
§2 CHECK 3 (tautology): identify tests where assertion matches a literal set up earlier in the test body; severity HIGH
§2 CHECK 4 (boundary): for security-critical functions (auth / validation / parser), zero adversarial tests is HIGH; report which tests cover boundary / failure / adversarial vs only happy path
§2 CHECK 5 (fake dependency): report any mock whose return is constant for any input (the mock tests the mock, not the function)
§2 CHECK 6 (lazy assertion): identify any toBeDefined / toBeTruthy / toBeGreaterThan(0) that wouldn't catch a wrong-but-truthy result; MEDIUM (not HIGH — depends on intent)
§2 CHECK 7 (flakiness): identify tests with Date.now / sleep / global state / order dependencies; HIGH if cross-test order matters
Output per-file score; any HIGH = auto-FAIL; engineer can re-run with --medium to also fail on MEDIUM
Findings table with test name + check + evidence + remediation

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer's test suite uses sqlite-in-memory for the database layer (a best practice). test-guard should NOT flag the DB mock as fake-dependency (it's a real test instance).
expected_behavior: Check 5 (fake dependency) recognizes sqlite-in-memory + testcontainers + wiremock as real test instances, not mocks
Only mocks that are 'constant return regardless of input' get flagged
For tests that DO mock the system under test (e.g. test mocks the parser it's supposed to test), flag regardless of the rest of the suite's quality

notes: Source adapted from antigravity test-guard skill. Complement to test-driven-development (TDD methodology) — test-guard enforces the resulting tests actually test what TDD claims they test. Per-PR CI integration: this skill's findings feed a PR gate that fails on HIGH-severity findings. Pair with ai-code-blindspots (cross-cutting AI blindspots) — test-guard is exclusively about test-code quality. Several existing ai-code-blindspots patterns are also test-guard triggers (over-mocking, toBeDefined lazy assertions); the two skills complement rather than overlap because ai-code-blindspots catches all AI-generated code patterns and test-guard specifically enumerates test-file landmines.
